title: What Is Bayesian Statistics?
tags:
resulting in a "posterior" distribution. this posterior distribution represents the refined belief about the parameter after incorporating the new evidence. this iterative updating process allows for continuous learning as more data accumulates.

examples

  • **medical diagnosis:** estimating the probability of a disease given a positive test result and the known prevalence of the disease.
  • **spam filtering:** calculating the probability that an email is spam based on the presence of certain keywords and the prior probability of an email being spam.
  • **machine learning:** used in algorithms like naive bayes classifiers for classification tasks.
  • **a/b testing:** analyzing the results of experiments by updating beliefs about the effectiveness of different versions.

why is it important? / significance

bayesian statistics is important because it provides a powerful and intuitive framework for statistical inference
